   - Mesh snapshot image present (not the "Mesh image unavailable" black box).
   - Aesthetic wheel rendered.
   - Metric rows: all metrics listed. **[KNOWN-BUG CHECK]** Check Skin quality and Expression asymmetry rows — expected bug: values formatted as percentages (e.g. "4.0% (≤ …%)") instead of the in-app texture/ratio format.
   - **[KNOWN-BUG CHECK]** With your long note from step 23: expected bug — notes (and possibly the metric table) clip off the bottom of the single page with no "continued" marker; footer always says "Page 1 of 1".
   - Confirm NO calibration warning strip (you are fully calibrated). Then clear calibration (More → Calibrate → Reset all), re-export, and confirm the amber warning strip APPEARS. Recalibrate after.
   - Note what's absent: clinical photos, region-change table, oblique meshes (stale-PDF gap — confirm for the record).
33. Export a visit whose label contains `/` and an emoji (save a quick extra case named `A/B 🧪`). Verify the share sheet still appears and the filename is sane.

## 8. Quick robustness spot-checks

34. Patient with 0 visits: create patient, open it — "No visits yet" card, no crash.
35. Multi-select compare with the dimmed/unreadable visit row if you ever see one — note behavior.
36. Rotate device, switch light/dark system mode, and background/foreground on every major screen (Capture, Analysis, Calibration, Compare, PDF share) — no layout breakage or crashes.

**Deliverable:** the numbered PASS/FAIL list, the noise-floor region values from step 29, screenshots of any failures, and the device model + iOS version.

audits.summary: The new clinical-photo storage has one genuine blocker: photos and meshes are stored via SwiftData `.externalStorage`, which writes blobs outside the store file, so the backup-exclusion code in FaceMapApp.swift silently fails to do what its own comment promises and patient face photos will travel into iCloud/Finder backups. Two further high-severity gaps compound this: the "Lock with Face ID" setting is decorative (nothing ever enforces it), and there is no app-switcher privacy redaction or share-sheet temp-file cleanup, so patient photos and PDFs linger in system snapshots and tmp. The disclaimer posture is strong and consistent in-app and in the PDF, with only modest drift on the web copy; the try? triage splits cleanly into 16 must-surface (real patient-data-loss paths around context.save and JSON encodes), 15 must-log, and 2 acceptable-silent sites.

findings.title: Clinical photos and meshes escape the iCloud-backup exclusion via .externalStorage
findings.detail: FaceMapApp.swift:33-48 excludes only the store file plus '-shm'/'-wal' sidecars from backup, and its comment claims patient data 'must not travel into iCloud / Finder backups'. But PatientCase.swift:18,31-39 marks capturedFaceJSON, both oblique meshes, and all three new photo JPEGs as @Attribute(.externalStorage). SwiftData/Core Data writes external binary blobs into a separate `<store>_SUPPORT/_EXTERNAL_DATA/` directory next to the store, which the suffix loop never touches. Net effect: the most sensitive data in the app — identifiable patient face photos — is now backed up to iCloud while only the comparatively small SQLite metadata is excluded. This directly contradicts README pre-production checklist item 3 and the privacy story implied to practitioners.
findings.recommendation: In excludeStoreFromBackup, also set isExcludedFromBackup on the external-storage support directory (e.g. enumerate configuration.url.deletingLastPathComponent() and exclude `\(storeName)_SUPPORT` / `.\(storeName)_SUPPORT` if present), or simpler and more robust: place the store in a dedicated subdirectory of Application Support via ModelConfiguration(url:) and exclude that whole directory. Add a unit/UI test that creates a case with a photo and asserts every file under the store directory has the exclusion flag.

findings.title: 'Lock with Face ID' setting is never enforced — false privacy promise over patient photos
findings.detail: SettingsScreen.swift:7 defines @AppStorage("biometricLockEnabled") and the section footer promises 'When on, FaceMap re-authenticates with Face ID before opening saved cases.' LAContext is used only inside SettingsScreen.swift:147-164 to authenticate the act of toggling the switch on. A repo-wide grep shows no other reference to biometricLockEnabled — PatientListScreen, PatientDetailScreen, AnalysisScreen, and the photos sheet never check it. A practitioner who enables the lock believes patient face photos are gated; they are not. A documented-but-unimplemented security control is worse than no control for a medical-adjacent app.
findings.recommendation: Either enforce it (e.g. a BiometricGate wrapper around ContentView or around PatientDetailScreen/AnalysisScreen that calls LAContext.evaluatePolicy with .deviceOwnerAuthentication on appear and on return from background when biometricLockEnabled is true) or remove the toggle until implemented. Prefer .deviceOwnerAuthentication (passcode fallback) over biometrics-only.

findings.title: Exported treatment-plan PDFs accumulate in tmp with no cleanup and no file protection
findings.detail: PDFShareItem.write (ShareSheet.swift:22-32) writes the PDF to FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory with only [.atomic], and nothing ever deletes it — ShareSheet (AnalysisScreen.swift:147-149) has no completionWithItemsHandler and the sheet's dismissal does no cleanup. tmp is purged only opportunistically by iOS, so PDFs containing patient code, visit label, metric values, annotation pins, and free-text clinical notes persist on disk indefinitely under the default NSFileProtectionCompleteUntilFirstUserAuthentication class. The filename itself (AnalysisScreen.swift:517, 'FaceMap_\(p.code)_\(c.label)_…') embeds the patient code, and sanitization only strips '/' (ShareSheet.swift:23), so a label containing ':' or control characters survives into the filename. Write failure returns nil silently and the practitioner just sees nothing happen.
findings.recommendation: Write each PDF to a unique subdirectory of tmp with options [.atomic, .completeFileProtection]; delete the file (or the whole subdirectory) in UIActivityViewController.completionWithItemsHandler and on sheet dismissal; sweep leftover FaceMap_*.pdf files from tmp at app launch; surface an alert when write fails. Broaden filename sanitization to an allowlist of [A-Za-z0-9._-].

findings.title: No app-switcher privacy redaction — patient face photos captured in system snapshots
findings.detail: A repo-wide grep finds no scenePhase handling, no .privacySensitive() usage, and no snapshot-redaction overlay anywhere. The new clinical-photos sheet (AnalysisScreen.swift:299-330) and the before/after photo row (ComparisonScreen.swift:132-156) display full patient face photos; when the practitioner swipes to the app switcher or backgrounds the app, iOS writes that screen to the on-disk snapshot cache and displays it in the switcher on a shared clinic device. This is the textbook leak vector for medical photo apps.
findings.recommendation: Add a scenePhase observer at the FaceMapApp/ContentView level that overlays an opaque brand splash (or applies .redacted/.privacySensitive) whenever scenePhase != .active. This is a ~20-line change and covers every screen at once, including photos, mesh, notes, and patient list.

findings.title: try? triage — 16 must-surface sites: silent loss of patient data on save/encode
findings.detail: These try? sites can destroy or fail to persist patient clinical data with zero user feedback. (a) ModelContext saves: CaseStore.swift:34, 48, 58, 64, 69, 75, 96 — archive/unarchive/createPatient/deletePatient/delete(case) all `try? context.save()`; note the one save that IS handled (CaseStore.swift:85-90) proves the team already has the logger pattern, the others just never got it. A failed delete at :96 is also a privacy bug (patient thinks data is gone; it is not). (b) AnalysisScreen.swift:142 (annotation pins) and :170 (clinical notes) — a practitioner's typed notes silently vanish if save fails. (c) PatientCase encodes: PatientCase.swift:56-57 fall back to empty Data(), silently discarding the entire frontal mesh/metric results at case creation; :60, :62, :63 silently drop annotations and both oblique captures (the new photo fields are raw Data so they are safe); :123 and :129 do the same on update.
findings.recommendation: Route all ModelContext.save() calls through one CaseStore.persist(_ operation: String) helper that does do/try/catch, logs via the existing redacting Logger, and publishes a user-visible error (e.g. @Published lastSaveError driving an alert/toast: 'Could not save — your last change was not stored'). Make PatientCase.init throwing (or failable) instead of substituting empty Data() for the mesh; a case that cannot encode must not be silently created hollow.

findings.title: try? triage — 15 must-log sites and 2 acceptable-silent sites
findings.detail: Must-log (failure is recoverable or already indirectly surfaced, but currently leaves no forensic trail): PatientCase.swift:88, 93, 98, 114, 119 — decode failures make a corrupt record render as an empty/missing case with no way to distinguish 'corrupt' from 'never captured'; CaseStore.swift:29 and :43 — fetch failures silently skip orphan migration or create a duplicate 'Unassigned' patient; CaseStore.swift:87 and :97 — cloud-sync upload/delete are stubs today, but :97 is pre-wired so that when sync goes live a failed cloud delete silently retains patient data server-side (a real privacy obligation failure — flag it now); SchemaMigrationPlan.swift:61 and :64 — a failed migration fetch/save silently leaves rows unmigrated; LandmarkCalibrationStore.swift:22, :45 and RegionCalibrationStore.swift:22, :45 — calibration decode/encode failures (covered in the calibration finding). Acceptable-silent (purely visual, nil is a sane fallback): FaceMeshOverlay.swift:220 and CalibrationMeshView.swift:225 — MeshResource.generate for an overlay entity.
findings.recommendation: Replace each must-log try? with do/catch + Logger (the redacting subsystem 'com.fuanne.facemap' already exists in CaseStore.swift:12). For PatientCase decode failures, additionally expose an isCorrupt flag so the UI can show 'Record unreadable' instead of an empty analysis. For CaseStore.swift:97, queue failed cloud deletes for retry rather than dropping them. Leave the two MeshResource sites as-is with a brief comment marking them intentionally silent.

findings.title: Calibration data in UserDefaults has no validation, versioning, or corruption notice
findings.detail: LandmarkCalibrationStore.swift:20-30 and RegionCalibrationStore.swift:20-30: if the stored JSON fails to decode, calibrated() silently returns [:], which flips isFullyCalibrated to false — the practitioner's entire calibration evaporates with no explanation beyond the uncalibrated banner reappearing, and the corrupt blob stays in place. save() at LandmarkCalibrationStore.swift:45 silently drops the write on encode failure (the Save button still dismisses, CalibrationScreen.swift:225-226). Saved indices are never range-validated against the mesh vertex count at save time (downstream consumers do bounds-check: AnalyzableFace.swift:30-34, SurfaceDisplacementMetric.swift:100, AsymmetryMetric.swift:98, SurfaceChangeAnalyzer.swift:60 — good), so a stale or hand-edited index degrades to 'landmark silently ignored' rather than a crash, which is itself a quiet clinical-accuracy failure. There is also no record of when calibration was performed or against which mesh topology, and UserDefaults restores across device migrations, which contradicts the disclaimer's 'calibrated on this device' framing (DisclaimerCopy.swift:15-17).
findings.recommendation: Wrap both stores' payloads in a small versioned envelope {version, savedAt, deviceModel, vertexCount, entries}; validate indices 0..<vertexCount at save time and reject otherwise; on decode failure, log and surface a one-time 'Calibration data was unreadable and has been reset' alert instead of silently returning [:]; show the calibration date in Settings and in the PDF next to the isCalibrated flag.

findings.title: PDF hardcodes Dr Nikolis as preparer and signatory for every practitioner
findings.detail: TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:104 prints 'Prepared by Dr Andreas Nikolis · MD, FRCSC' and :248 prints 'Practitioner signature — Dr Andreas Nikolis, MD, FRCSC' on every exported plan, regardless of who actually uses the app. The first-launch gate (DisclaimerGate.swift:45) attests that the user is 'a licensed practitioner', not that they are Dr Nikolis. Any other practitioner exporting a plan produces a clinical document misattributed to a named physician — a professional-liability and regulatory problem the moment the app reaches a second user, and it weakens the otherwise consistent 'practitioner is the sole clinical decision-maker' posture (DisclaimerCopy.swift:48-50, correctly present in the footer at TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:266).
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Practitioner name & credentials' field in SettingsScreen (UserDefaults) and use it for both the header and signature line, defaulting to blank ('Prepared with FaceMap') rather than to Dr Nikolis. Keep the Nikolis framework attribution in the About copy where it already lives (DisclaimerCopy.aboutNikolis).

findings.title: No Data Protection entitlement — patient store readable after first unlock
findings.detail: There is no .entitlements file in the repo and FaceMap.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj contains no CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS or file-protection build setting, so all app files (SwiftData store, external photo blobs, tmp PDFs) sit at the default NSFileProtectionCompleteUntilFirstUserAuthentication class: decryptable any time after the first unlock since boot, including while the device is locked in a clinic. For an app holding identifiable patient face photos, NSFileProtectionComplete (or at minimum CompleteUnlessOpen for files written in background) is the appropriate baseline and is nearly free to adopt since the app is foreground-only.
findings.recommendation: Add the Data Protection capability with NSFileProtectionComplete as the default class in the entitlements file; verify ARKit capture and SwiftData writes still succeed (the app never writes in background, so Complete is viable). Pair with the tmp-PDF .completeFileProtection fix.

findings.title: Web disclaimer drifted from iOS source despite 'verbatim' contract
findings.detail: web/content/disclaimer.ts:1-4 declares 'Verbatim from FaceMap/UI/DisclaimerCopy.swift. Do not edit without changing the iOS source first.' Two drifts: (1) the file path is stale — the source now lives at FaceMap/Resources/DisclaimerCopy.swift; (2) the iOS firstLaunchBody gained a third paragraph ('Metric outputs are not clinically meaningful until the landmark indices have been calibrated…', DisclaimerCopy.swift:15-17) that web/content/disclaimer.ts:8-12 lacks, so the public /legal/disclaimer page (web/app/legal/disclaimer/page.tsx) shows weaker, outdated language than the app. The core not-a-medical-device line itself is consistent everywhere checked: DisclaimerCopy.swift:6-10, PDF footer TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:266, web layout meta (web/app/layout.tsx:34), methodology page (:147), terms page (:21), and disclaimer-banner.tsx:15 — that posture is solid.
findings.recommendation: Sync disclaimer.ts with the current Swift copy (add the calibration paragraph, fix the path comment) and add a tiny CI check or script that diffs the two so 'verbatim' stays true; consider exporting the disclaimer strings from one JSON source consumed by both targets.

findings.title: Camera purpose string predates clinical-photo capture
findings.detail: FaceMap/Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription says the camera is used 'to construct a 3D face model for aesthetic analysis' — written before v0.5. The app now also stores full clinical photographs per pose (FaceCaptureView.swift:84-94). Apple reviews purpose strings for accuracy, and more importantly the patient-consent attestation in the disclaimer ('the patient has consented to having their face captured', DisclaimerCopy.swift:19-20) is anchored to what the practitioner believes is captured; storing identifiable photos under a mesh-only description understates the data collected. The App Store privacy nutrition label will likewise need to declare photo collection.
findings.recommendation: Update NSCameraUsageDescription to mention both the 3D mesh and clinical reference photographs stored with each visit, e.g. 'FaceMap uses the TrueDepth camera to capture a 3D face model and clinical reference photos for practitioner planning. Data stays on this device.'

findings.title: Disclaimer acceptance is an unversioned, untimestamped boolean
findings.detail: DisclaimerGate.swift:4 stores acceptance as @AppStorage("hasAcceptedDisclaimerV1") = true. There is no record of when it was accepted or which copy revision was shown, and the practitioner/consent attestation ('I am a licensed practitioner — continue', DisclaimerGate.swift:45) is the app's main regulatory shield. If the disclaimer text materially changes (it already did once when the calibration paragraph was added without bumping V1), existing users never see the new terms. For a defensible audit trail you want at least {acceptedAt, copyVersion}.
findings.recommendation: Store an acceptance record (date + a disclaimerVersion constant bumped whenever firstLaunchBody materially changes) and re-present the gate when the stored version is older than the current one. Show the acceptance date in More → About.

synthesis.executiveSummary: Five auditors converge on one verdict: FaceMap's single-visit experience is polished, but the differentiating longitudinal journey is broken end-to-end. All five flagged variants of the same blocker — "Add visit" never binds a capture to its patient (PatientDetailScreen.swift:89-98 → AnalysisScreen.swift:442-459 → CaseStore.swift:80-84), the promised re-bind UI doesn't exist, and CaptureScreen never resets between sessions, risking cross-patient mesh mixing. Security found a second genuine blocker: clinical photos stored via .externalStorage escape the iCloud-backup exclusion in FaceMapApp.swift:33-48, and the Face ID lock toggle is decorative. The flagship ComparisonScreen renders surface displacement in wrong units (2mm shows as "0.2%") and has no export, while the PDF is stale, single-page, mis-formatted, and misattributes every report to Dr Nikolis. Wave 2 is organized into 7 disjoint work orders: (P0) core patient workflow + capture reliability, comparison correctness + PDF v2, privacy/security enforcement; (P1) data-integrity hardening + edge-case tests, design-system/brand/copy alignment across iOS and web, asset catalog + app icon; (P2) onboarding + disclaimer versioning. Deferred: encrypted local backup, structured treatment records, Data Protection entitlement, and Dynamic Type — each with reasons. Human actions center on on-device verification (especially the noise-floor check and verified ARKit landmark indices) and Dr Nikolis copy sign-off.

workOrders.title: Core patient workflow: visit binding, capture reliability, case lifecycle
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap is an iOS app (SwiftUI + SwiftData + ARKit TrueDepth) for aesthetic-medicine practitioners. Its headline value is visit-over-visit outcome tracking per patient, but that journey is broken. Fix the following in the listed files ONLY. IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS: (1) do NOT create new .swift files — the Xcode project uses explicit PBXFileReference entries and project.pbxproj is owned by another work order; add new types inside the files you own. (2) Any new SwiftData model fields must be optional (lightweight migration) — but prefer no schema changes here. (3) Use existing Theme/Type design tokens (FaceMap/UI/DesignSystem/Theme.swift, Typography.swift) for all new UI. (4) Never alter the app's not-a-medical-device framing or disclaimer copy.

1. PATIENT BINDING (blocker): PatientDetailScreen.swift:89-98 pushes a bare CaptureScreen() with a false comment 'binding handled in CaptureScreen save'. CaptureScreen takes no patient; AnalysisScreen's save sheet (AnalysisScreen.swift:442-459) creates PatientCase with patient == nil, and CaseStore.save (CaseStore.swift:80-84) silently dumps it into the 'Unassigned' bucket. Thread an optional `patient: Patient?` through CaptureScreen → AnalysisScreen → the save sheet. When present: pre-fill/lock the patient, auto-suggest label 'Visit N' (N = existing case count + 1), set pc.patient before store.save.

2. SAVE SHEET REDESIGN: The sheet's TextField is titled 'Patient code (no PII)' with example 'P-014 Visit 2' (AnalysisScreen.swift:430-433) but the text is stored as a case label and the patient is ignored. Replace with: an explicit patient picker (existing active patients + 'New patient…' inline + 'Unassigned' default when no patient was threaded) and a separate optional 'Visit label' field (footer e.g. 'Visit 2 — pre-treatment') defaulting to 'Visit N'.

3. RE-BIND (blocker): The empty state at PatientListScreen.swift:85 promises 'an Unassigned bucket you can re-bind later' but no re-bind UI exists. Implement CaseStore.reassign(_ case:to patient:) and add 'Move to patient…' via context menu on visit rows in PatientDetailScreen and a toolbar item in AnalysisScreen when existingCase != nil, presenting a patient picker with 'New patient' inline.

4. CAPTURE SESSION RESET (blocker): CaptureScreen's @State (captures, photos, currentPose, poseInWindowSince) is never cleared after the 3-pose sequence completes (CaptureScreen.swift:78-96). Because the NavigationStack keeps the view alive, a later session leaves currentPose at .obliqueR with stale frontal/obliqueL meshes — the next snapshot mixes poses from two different visits (clinically dangerous cross-patient data). Reset all capture state once a MultiPoseCapture is finalized (or in .onAppear when all poses are filled). Add a 'Start over' button and per-pose retake (tapping a completed segment in the progress bar recaptures that pose).

5. CAMERA PERMISSION / AR SESSION FAILURE: FaceCaptureView.swift never checks AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus and the Coordinator (FaceCaptureView.swift:41-62, also see FaceCaptureCoordinator.swift) implements no session(_:didFailWithError:), sessionWasInterrupted, or sessionInterruptionEnded. Denied camera leaves 'Position your face in front of the camera' forever (CaptureScreen.swift:171-172). Add a pre-flight authorization check and the three missing ARSessionDelegate methods; introduce .permissionDenied / .sessionFailed / .interrupted TrackingState cases that CaptureScreen renders as an explanatory card with an 'Open Settings' button (UIApplication.openSettingsURLString). On interruption/failure, clear the frame buffer (recentGeometries) and pendingSnapshot; reset poseInWindowSince/isCapturing when scenePhase != .active; re-verify the yaw window at snapshot time and drop snapshots whose yaw left the window. Also: guard the frame-averaging buffer so only samples with vertices.count == recentGeometries[0].vertices.count are averaged (FaceCaptureView.swift:68-72), and when currentFramePhotoJPEG returns nil (FaceCaptureView.swift:90-95) surface a non-blocking warning in CaptureScreen ('Mesh captured; clinical photo failed — retake?').

6. SAVE FAILURE SURFACING: CaseStore.save catches context.save() errors and only logs (CaseStore.swift:85-91) while the save sheet dismisses unconditionally (AnalysisScreen.swift:456-458). Make CaseStore.save throw (or return Result); on failure keep the sheet open and show an alert ('Could not save this visit — try again'). Route ALL remaining `try? context.save()` sites in CaseStore (lines 34, 48, 58, 64, 69, 75, 96) through one persist(_ operation:) helper that does do/try/catch, logs via the existing redacting Logger (subsystem 'com.fuanne.facemap', CaseStore.swift:12), and exposes a published lastSaveError driving an alert. Apply the same to notes/annotation saves in AnalysisScreen.swift:142 and :170. Debounce the per-keystroke notes save (1-2s debounce + save on sheet dismiss) and cap notes at 20,000 chars with a counter.

7. ARCHIVE SAFETY: Swipe-to-delete archives with no confirmation (PatientListScreen.swift:32-34), the menu 'Archive patient' likewise (PatientDetailScreen.swift:55-57), and CaseStore.unarchive/deletePatient (CaseStore.swift:67-76) are never called from any view. Use custom .swipeActions labeled 'Archive' (non-destructive tint, not red Delete), confirm before archiving, and add an 'Archived' section/filter in PatientListScreen exposing Unarchive and permanent Delete (with confirmation).

8. VISIT LIFECYCLE: CaseStore.delete(patientCase:) (CaseStore.swift:93-98) is never invoked. Add swipe-to-delete (with confirmation) and 'Rename visit' context-menu actions on visit rows in PatientDetailScreen.

9. COMPARE DISCOVERABILITY: Comparison currently requires ellipsis menu → 'Compare visits' → multi-select exactly two → small 'Compare 2/2' link (PatientDetailScreen.swift:48-53, 232-239). Add a prominent 'Compare with previous' button on the latest-visit card whenever ≥2 visits exist (pre-selecting the two most recent); keep multi-select for arbitrary pairs; remove the unreachable fallback string 'Pick exactly two visits to compare.' (PatientDetailScreen.swift:69).

10. VALIDATION & SEARCH: CaseStore.createPatient substitutes 'Untitled' silently and allows duplicate codes (CaseStore.swift:54-59), as does the rename sheet (PatientDetailScreen.swift:327-353) — show inline 'This code is already in use'. Extend search (PatientListScreen.swift:71-75) to match visit labels and notes, show matching-visit subtitles, and add a 'No patients match "<query>"' empty state.

11. COPY FIXES in owned files: replace the '<domains> metrics arrive in v0.3.' footnote (AnalysisScreen.swift:254) with 'No <domain> metrics for this capture'; replace 'Coming soon' trend-card text (PatientDetailScreen.swift:180) with 'No data yet'. Add an isEvaluating loading state to AnalysisScreen so a 'Analyzing…' placeholder shows on first metric computation and stale findings are cleared/dimmed during pose-switch recomputes (AnalysisScreen.swift:150-154, 203, 415-422). When face.vertexCount is 0 (or < 100), show 'Capture invalid — please recapture' and disable Save. For the PDF export button's silent failures (AnalysisScreen.swift:510-519), alert 'Could not generate the PDF for this visit' on nil patient / nil data / write failure and show a brief progress overlay. Finally, stop using Theme.domainSymmetry (pink, a clinical facet hue) for the pose chip and unsupported-device badge in CaptureScreen.swift:133,163 — use Theme.ink/inkMuted for the chip and an inline amber Color for the error badge with a '// TODO: migrate to Theme.warning token' comment.
acceptanceCriteria: Starting capture from PatientDetailScreen 'Add visit' for patient P-001 and saving lands the visit in P-001's timeline (not Unassigned), with label defaulting to 'Visit N'
An Unassigned case can be moved to an existing or newly created patient via 'Move to patient…' from both PatientDetailScreen visit rows and a saved case's AnalysisScreen toolbar
After completing a 3-pose session, re-entering CaptureScreen shows a fully reset state (no filled progress segments); a 'Start over' control and per-pose retake exist
With camera permission denied, CaptureScreen shows an explanatory card with a working 'Open Settings' button instead of 'Position your face…'
A failing CaseStore.save keeps the save sheet open and shows an error alert; no try? context.save() remains in CaseStore.swift
Archiving requires confirmation, uses an 'Archive' (not 'Delete') swipe action, and archived patients are listable, unarchivable, and permanently deletable with confirmation
Visits can be deleted (with confirmation) and renamed from PatientDetailScreen
A 'Compare with previous' one-tap affordance appears whenever a patient has ≥2 visits
Duplicate patient codes are rejected with inline feedback on create and rename; search matches visit labels/notes and shows a no-results state
No user-visible string mentions 'v0.3' or 'Coming soon' in the edited files; project builds (xcodebuild -scheme FaceMap build) and existing FaceMapTests pass

workOrders.title: Data-integrity hardening and edge-case test suite
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap (iOS, SwiftData persistence, ARKit 1220-vertex face meshes) stores patient meshes as JSON blobs and calibration data in UserDefaults. The persistence seams are fragile: structurally-valid-but-corrupt data can hard-crash the app. Fix and test the following, in the listed files ONLY. CONSTRAINTS: (1) do NOT create new .swift files — the Xcode project uses explicit file references and project.pbxproj is owned by another work order; add new XCTestCase classes inside the existing test files listed below (multiple test classes per file is fine). (2) Do NOT change public initializer signatures that other files call (e.g. PatientCase.init is called from AnalysisScreen, which another agent owns) — validate and log instead. (3) Any new SwiftData fields must be optional. (4) Do not touch UI files.

1. CapturedFace (FaceMap/Capture/CapturedFace.swift): synthesized Codable accepts any [Float]/[Int16]. The `vertices` accessor strides vertexData by 3 with no multiple-of-3 check (lines 39-48) — a truncated array traps; `transform` indexes d[0]…d[15] with no count check (lines 50-58); FaceMeshOverlay.swift:219 maps triangle indices with UInt32($0) — any negative Int16 traps. Write a custom init(from decoder:) that validates vertexData.count % 3 == 0, transformData.count == 16, triangleIndices.count % 3 == 0, and all indices in 0..<vertexCount, throwing DecodingError otherwise so failures flow into the existing nil → 'Mesh unreadable' paths (PatientCase.capturedFace returns nil on decode failure, PatientCase.swift:88). Keep encoding unchanged for backward compatibility.

2. PatientCase (FaceMap/Cases/PatientCase.swift): decode failures at lines 88, 93, 98, 114, 119 are silent `try?` — replace with do/catch + Logger (subsystem 'com.fuanne.facemap') so corrupt records leave a forensic trail, and expose a computed `isCorrupt`-style indicator (decodes attempted but failed) the UI can adopt later. The encode fallbacks at lines 56-57 substitute empty Data() for the entire mesh — log these as errors too (do not change the init signature this wave).

3. Calibration stores (FaceMap/Analysis/Landmarks/LandmarkCalibrationStore.swift, RegionCalibrationStore.swift): save() and calibrated() perform no validation (lines ~20-30, 43-49). Validate indices to 0..<1220 on both read and write, dropping invalid entries; wrap payloads in a small versioned envelope {version, savedAt, deviceModel, vertexCount, entries} with backward-compatible decoding of the current unversioned format; log (not silently swallow) decode/encode failures at lines 22 and 45 of each store.

4. CalibrationMeshView (FaceMap/Visualization/CalibrationMeshView.swift): the picked-index guard checks only the upper bound (`idx < verts.count` at lines 151-153, `center < verts.count` at 171-172) — add `idx >= 0` / `center >= 0` so a negative persisted index cannot crash the calibration screen. Leave the MeshResource.generate try? at CalibrationMeshView.swift:225 and FaceMeshOverlay.swift:220 silent but add a brief '// intentionally silent: visual-only fallback' comment.

5. SchemaMigrationPlan.swift: replace the silent try? at lines 61 and 64 with do/catch + Logger (a failed migration fetch/save must not be invisible).

6. FaceLandmarkIndices.swift: the default seed indices (lines 35-58: 168 nasion, 133/362/33/263 canthi, 234/454 zygions) are canonical MediaPipe FaceMesh (468-vertex) indices, not ARKit 1220-vertex topology. Add a prominent doc comment stating the seeds are MediaPipe-derived, unverified on ARKit topology, and that uncalibrated values measure essentially arbitrary points (this also drives SurfaceChangeAnalyzer's stable-landmark offset cancellation).

7. TESTS — add these as new XCTestCase classes inside the existing files listed (do not create new files): in PatientMigrationTests.swift: CapturedFace Codable round-trip; corrupt payloads (truncated vertexData, 15-float transform, negative triangle indices) decode to throw/nil rather than trap; PatientCase with empty Data → capturedFace == nil and metricResults == []; garbage JSON → nil; multiPoseCapture reassembly (PatientCase.swift:103-111). In LandmarkCalibrationStoreTests.swift and RegionCalibrationStoreTests.swift: negative/huge indices rejected; corrupt Data under the UserDefaults key degrades gracefully with logging; versioned-envelope round-trip and legacy-format read. In MetricRegistryTests.swift: evaluateAll on a zero-vertex AnalyzableFace returns every metric's NaN/'Unavailable' failure result without trapping. In SurfaceChangeAnalyzerTests.swift: pin noiseFloorMeters == 0.0003 and test exceedsNoiseFloor boundary behavior at exactly 0.3mm. Also add CapturePose window-math tests (contains/yawError at ±5° boundaries, sign conventions) and HeadPose.from(transform:) Euler decomposition tests (identity and 30°-yaw transforms) — place these classes in SurfaceChangeAnalyzerTests.swift or MetricRegistryTests.swift. Use/extend SyntheticFace.swift helpers as needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Feeding truncated vertexData, a 15-float transform, or negative triangle indices through JSONDecoder produces a thrown DecodingError (never a runtime trap), verified by passing unit tests
A valid pre-existing CapturedFace JSON blob still decodes identically (round-trip test passes)

Both calibration stores reject out-of-range/negative indices on save and read, survive corrupt Data under their UserDefaults keys, and persist a versioned envelope while still reading the legacy format
CalibrationMeshView bounds guards include the negative case

No silent try? remains in PatientCase decode paths, SchemaMigrationPlan, or the calibration stores (all log via the com.fuanne.facemap Logger); the two MeshResource.generate sites carry an 'intentionally silent' comment
noiseFloorMeters == 0.0003 is pinned by a test; zero-vertex evaluateAll, CapturePose boundary, and HeadPose decomposition tests pass
No new .swift files were created; full test suite passes via xcodebuild test

workOrders.title: Comparison correctness + before/after report and PDF v2
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap differentiates from 2D competitors on 3D visit-over-visit comparison, but the ComparisonScreen mis-formats units and the PDF (TreatmentPlanPDF) predates the current feature set (clinical photos per pose, region projection changes, 8 metrics including photo-based skin quality). Rebuild reporting in the listed files ONLY. CONSTRAINTS: (1) do NOT create new .swift files — the Xcode project uses explicit file references and project.pbxproj is owned elsewhere; add new types (MetricValueFormatter, ComparisonReportPDF, PDFType) inside the Reports files you own. (2) Do NOT edit AnalysisScreen.swift or SurfaceChangeAnalyzer.swift (owned by other agents) — read them for reference only. (3) The not-a-medical-device disclaimer footer must remain on every PDF page. (4) Match Theme/Type design tokens in on-screen UI.

1. SHARED METRIC FORMATTER (P0 bug): ComparisonScreen.formatValue (ComparisonScreen.swift:329-336) special-cases only canthal tilt and asymmetry; SurfaceDisplacementMetric (meters), ExpressionAsymmetryMetric (unitless ratio), and the skin-quality texture index fall into a default '%.1f%%' branch that multiplies by 100 — a 2mm projection deficit renders as '0.2%'. Create a MetricValueFormatter type (place it in PDFTheme.swift or TreatmentPlanPDF.swift) covering all 8 metric ids, mirroring the correct per-metric formats in AnalysisScreen.formatValue (AnalysisScreen.swift:536-560 — read, don't edit). Use it in ComparisonScreen and both PDFs. formatPDFValue (TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:287-297) has the same wrong-units bug — replace it with the shared formatter.

2. PDF v2 (TreatmentPlanPDF.swift + PDFTheme.swift): currently one fixed A4 page (begins exactly one PDF page, TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:30-38; footer hardcodes 'Page 1 of 1' at :270), relies on a Spacer (:81) so content silently compresses/clips, shows only a black mesh snapshot (:128-141) and no photos. Rebuild as multi-page (loop context.beginPDFPage): PAGE 1 (patient-facing): header (brand mark, practitioner, patient code, visit date); 3-up clinical-photo triptych (oblique L / frontal / oblique R from PatientCase.photos, ~160pt tall, 6pt radius, captions; graceful placeholder when a photo is missing); aesthetic wheel with adequate bleed margin so labels at radius*1.02 don't clip; 'Key findings' box with the top 3 non-normal metrics in plain language; signature block. PAGE 2 (clinical detail): metric table grouped by FAS facet with facet-hue tab per group, unit-correct values via MetricValueFormatter; 'Change since last visit' region-projection table (computed via SurfaceChangeAnalyzer when a prior visit for the same patient exists) with the ±0.3mm noise-floor note; annotation pins; notes (paginating to page 3+ instead of clipping); calibration strip and the existing disclaimer footer on EVERY page. Add a PDFType enum in PDFTheme.swift (display 24 serif tracked, sectionHeader 9 semibold tracked, body 10, metricName 10.5 serif, value 10 mono, caption 9, legal 8.5 minimum) and migrate all ~15 hardcoded .system(size:) calls; disclaimer/legal text must never render below 8.5pt.

3. PRACTITIONER ATTRIBUTION (liability): TreatmentPlanPDF.swift:104 hardcodes 'Prepared by Dr Andreas Nikolis · MD, FRCSC' and :248 a Nikolis signature line for every user. Read a practitioner name from UserDefaults key "practitionerName" (a Settings field for it is being added by another work order under exactly that key); when empty, render 'Prepared with FaceMap' and a blank signature line — never default to Dr Nikolis. Also replace the hardcoded calibration-warning amber at :235-238 with hex 0xB45309 ink on 0xFEF3C7 background (matching the in-app banner) with a '// TODO: Theme.warning token' comment.

4. COMPARISON REPORT: ComparisonScreen has no export. Add a ComparisonReportPDF generator (new type inside TreatmentPlanPDF.swift or PDFTheme.swift): side-by-side frontal photos, region projection-change table with noise-floor note, metric A→B delta table, day count, disclaimer footer. Add a share/export toolbar button on ComparisonScreen using the existing ShareSheet, with an error alert and progress overlay on failure (never a silent no-op).

5. COMPARISON UX CORRECTNESS (ComparisonScreen.swift): (a) Delta salience is inverted at :286-295 — improved metrics get near-black ink (max weight) while worsened get a pastel facet hue. Use inline status colors: worsened = 0x9B3B2E desaturated brick, improved = 0x3E7C4F desaturated green, unchanged = Theme.inkMuted, paired with the existing 'Improved'/'Worsened' text labels; never use facet/domain hues (including Theme.domainSymmetry at :202 for 'projection lost') for status. (b) Region-change analysis silently hides on failure: the .task skips when either mesh is nil (:38-43) and SurfaceChangeAnalyzer returns [] on topology mismatch, with the section rendering only if non-empty (:165-167). Track a tri-state (loading / computed / incompatible) and render an explicit 'These two captures cannot be compared (mesh missing or topology mismatch)' card. (c) The photo row requires BOTH frontal photos (:134-148) — show it when EITHER exists, with a 'No photo recorded for this visit' placeholder on the missing side. (d) Add the existing CalibrationWarningBanner component to ComparisonScreen when LandmarkCalibrationStore.shared.isFullyCalibrated is false (region tracking depends on calibrated landmarks).

6. SHARE-SHEET SECURITY (ShareSheet.swift): PDFShareItem.write (:22-32) writes to tmp with only [.atomic], never deletes, and sanitizes only '/'. Write to a unique tmp subdirectory with [.atomic, .completeFileProtection]; delete the file in UIActivityViewController.completionWithItemsHandler and on dismissal; sweep leftover FaceMap_*.pdf from tmp at first use; sanitize filenames to allowlist [A-Za-z0-9._-]; return a surfaced error (not silent nil) on write failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Surface displacement renders in mm and expression asymmetry as a unitless ratio in the ComparisonScreen delta table, matching AnalysisScreen's formats exactly for all 8 metric ids
TreatmentPlanPDF.generate produces ≥2 pages for a full case; 10,000-char notes paginate instead of clipping; the disclaimer footer appears on every page at ≥8.5pt

PDF page 1 contains the three clinical photos (or labeled placeholders) and a key-findings box; page 2 contains the facet-grouped metric table and, when a prior visit exists, a region-projection-change table with the ±0.3mm noise-floor note
No exported PDF mentions Dr Nikolis as preparer/signatory unless 'practitionerName' in UserDefaults is set to that value
ComparisonScreen has a working share/export button producing a before/after PDF, with a visible error alert on failure
Worsened deltas are visually heavier than improved ones using the brick/green status colors; no facet hue is used for status meaning

Incompatible/missing-mesh comparisons show an explicit explanatory card; a one-sided missing frontal photo shows a placeholder instead of hiding the row; the calibration banner appears on ComparisonScreen when uncalibrated
Exported PDFs are written with complete file protection, deleted after the share sheet completes, and filenames contain only [A-Za-z0-9._-]
Project builds and existing tests pass; no new .swift files created

workOrders.title: Privacy and security enforcement: backup exclusion, biometric lock, snapshot redaction
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap stores identifiable patient face photos and 3D meshes on-device for medical practitioners. Three enforcement gaps must be closed in the listed files ONLY. CONSTRAINTS: (1) do NOT create new .swift files (Xcode project uses explicit file references; project.pbxproj is owned elsewhere) — add new views/types inside FaceMapApp.swift. (2) Do not edit ContentView.swift, DisclaimerGate.swift, or any other UI file (owned by other agents); wrap at the FaceMapApp/WindowGroup level. (3) Do not weaken the not-a-medical-device framing.

1. BACKUP EXCLUSION (blocker): FaceMapApp.swift:33-48 excludes only the store file plus '-shm'/'-wal' sidecars from iCloud/Finder backup, but PatientCase marks the mesh blobs AND all three clinical photo JPEGs as @Attribute(.externalStorage) (PatientCase.swift:18,31-39 — read only, don't edit). SwiftData/Core Data writes external blobs to a separate '<store>_SUPPORT/_EXTERNAL_DATA/' directory next to the store, which the suffix loop never touches — so patient face photos currently travel into iCloud backups while only SQLite metadata is excluded. Fix robustly: relocate the store into a dedicated subdirectory of Application Support via ModelConfiguration(url:) and set isExcludedFromBackup on that whole directory (handling first-run migration of an existing store directory's files into the new location), or at minimum enumerate the store's parent directory and exclude the '_SUPPORT' / external-data directories and any other sidecars. Log (Logger subsystem 'com.fuanne.facemap') every path excluded and any failure. In DEBUG builds, assert after a save that every file under the store directory carries the exclusion flag.

2. BIOMETRIC LOCK ENFORCEMENT: SettingsScreen.swift:7 defines @AppStorage("biometricLockEnabled") with footer copy promising re-authentication 'before opening saved cases', but the flag is read nowhere else — LAContext is only used to authenticate toggling the switch (SettingsScreen.swift:147-164). Add a BiometricGate view inside FaceMapApp.swift wrapping the root content: when biometricLockEnabled is true, require LAContext.evaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthentication) (passcode fallback, not biometrics-only) on launch and whenever the scene returns to .active after backgrounding; until authenticated, show an opaque brand-styled lock screen (use Theme tokens) with a 'Unlock' retry button. Handle evaluatePolicy errors gracefully (e.g. passcode not set → show explanation, do not brick the app).

3. APP-SWITCHER REDACTION: No scenePhase handling or privacy redaction exists anywhere; patient face photos on screen (clinical-photos sheet, comparison before/after) are written into the iOS app-switcher snapshot. Add a scenePhase observer at the WindowGroup level that overlays an opaque brand splash (logo on Theme canvas) whenever scenePhase != .active. This covers all screens at once and composes with the biometric gate.

4. SETTINGS ADDITIONS (SettingsScreen.swift): add a 'Practitioner name & credentials' text field persisted to UserDefaults key exactly "practitionerName" (the PDF work order reads this key; default empty — never default to 'Dr Andreas Nikolis'), with a footer noting it appears on exported reports. Fix the stale cloud-sync copy at SettingsScreen.swift:84: 'Coming in Phase 2' → 'Planned — Phase 5'. Keep the sync stub disabled.

5. CAMERA PURPOSE STRING (Info.plist): NSCameraUsageDescription predates v0.5's clinical-photo capture and mentions only the 3D model. Update to: 'FaceMap uses the TrueDepth camera to capture a 3D face model and clinical reference photos for practitioner planning. Data stays on this device.'
acceptanceCriteria: After saving a case with photos, every file under the store's directory tree (including external-storage blob directories) has isExcludedFromBackup == true, verified by a DEBUG assertion/log walk
An existing store from the previous location/layout is still readable after the change (no data loss on upgrade)
With biometricLockEnabled on, relaunching or returning from background requires device-owner authentication before any patient data is visible; with it off, behavior is unchanged
Backgrounding the app on any screen shows an opaque brand overlay in the app switcher (no patient photos in snapshots)
SettingsScreen has a practitioner-name field persisted under the exact key 'practitionerName', defaulting to empty
NSCameraUsageDescription mentions both the 3D model and clinical reference photos and on-device storage; sync copy says Phase 5
Project builds; no new .swift files created; no edits outside the three listed files

workOrders.title: Design-system, brand, and copy alignment across iOS and web
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap's brand is a 5-facet FAS (Facial Assessment Scale) wheel, but iOS and web disagree on facet colors, the wheel double-books a quadrant, three screens bypass the design system, and About/disclaimer copy contradicts the 5-facet framework. Fix in the listed files ONLY. CONSTRAINTS: (1) do NOT create new .swift files (explicit-file-reference Xcode project; pbxproj owned elsewhere). (2) Do NOT edit AnalysisScreen, CaptureScreen, ComparisonScreen, PatientDetailScreen, SettingsScreen, DisclaimerGate, or Reports files (owned by other agents). (3) The not-a-medical-device disclaimer meaning must not change; copy fixes below are factual corrections only. (4) FaceDomain has 5 cases: skinQuality, facialShape, proportions, symmetry, expression.

1. COLOR UNIFICATION (web is source of truth): web/lib/tokens.ts:36-41 facetHues define skinQuality=#C9BBEE (lavender), facialShape=#A6B4DD, proportions=#7A8094 (slate), symmetry=#E9B5E0, expression=#F2C9A1 (peach). iOS Theme.swift:31-36 currently has skinQuality=0x7A8094 and expression=0xC9BBEE (swapped/wrong) and proportions=0x9AB2D6 (exists nowhere on web). Update Theme.swift to: domainSkinQuality=0xC9BBEE, domainFacialShape=0xA6B4DD, domainProportions=0x7A8094, domainSymmetry=0xE9B5E0, domainExpression=0xF2C9A1. Add mirror-pointer comments in both Theme.swift and tokens.ts ('keep in sync with <other file>'). Do not change web hex values.

2. STATUS TOKENS: Theme has no semantic status colors, so screens steal facet hues for status. Add to Theme.swift: warning=0xB45309, warningBg=0xFEF3C7, warningInk=0x78350F, positive=0x3E7C4F, negative=0x9B3B2E, plus a comment forbidding facet hues for non-facet meaning. Migrate CalibrationWarningBanner.swift:11-29 (currently hardcodes those ambers) to the tokens, and strengthen its copy to state values are 'not anatomically meaningful until calibrated'. Use positive for the 'picked' confirmation in CalibrationScreen.swift:150 and RegionCalibrationScreen.swift:156 (currently system .green).

3. FIVE-SECTOR WHEEL: AestheticWheel.swift:196-207 maps BOTH .skinQuality and .expression to the (180,270) quadrant — their gradient fills overpaint each other, labelOverlay (:156-171) renders 'SKIN QUALITY' and 'EXPRESSION' on top of each other (including in the exported PDF), and domain(forAngle:) (:219-228) always resolves that tap to .skinQuality, making Expression unreachable from the wheel. Implement five 72° sectors anchored with Skin Quality starting at 12 o'clock, in FaceDomain.allCases order; update quadrantBoundsDegrees, domain(forAngle:), and label placement (slight radial offset or adjusted anchors so labels at 72° spacing don't collide; ensure labels fit within the declared frame so PDF rendering doesn't clip at radius*1.02). Keep the 3-ring severity geometry untouched. Update LogoMark.swift to a matching 5-slice mark and fix its stale doc comment (lines 3-10 reference deleted tokens domainOptical/domainStructural/domainMechanical); update WheelGlyph.swift if it renders quadrants. Update web/components/brand-mark.tsx to five 72° arcs using facetHues (it currently draws 4 quadrants from an old domainHues object, lines 13-16).

4. TYPE SCALE: Typography.swift bottoms out at 11pt, so components hardcode micro sizes. Add Type.micro (9pt semibold, tracked), Type.labelSmall (10pt semibold), Type.button (16pt medium), Type.control (14pt semibold). Migrate hardcoded .system(size:) call sites within files you own: AestheticWheel.swift:167,178; DomainBadge.swift:15; SummaryHeader.swift:46; BrandMark.swift:54; MetricRow.swift:35 and CategoryRow.swift:60 (12pt semibold duplicating captionStrong); Buttons.swift:6,22; MeshFullScreen.swift:33.

5. OFF-SYSTEM SCREENS: CalibrationScreen.swift, RegionCalibrationScreen.swift, and MeshFullScreen.swift use zero Theme/Type tokens (system blue .borderedProminent buttons, raw Color.cyan at CalibrationScreen.swift:121, system fonts). Migrate them to Type.* fonts, Theme ink/surface tokens, and the existing PrimaryButton/SecondaryButton styles; mesh-viewport overlays may keep white-on-black but must use Type sizes. While in CalibrationScreen: 'Reset all' (lines 218-223) clears the persistent calibration store immediately even if the user backs out without saving — gate it behind a confirmation dialog and defer the store clear until Save.

6. SEVERITY REDUNDANCY: SeverityDot encodes severity by hue+opacity alone (SeverityDot.swift:10-16; mild = facet hue at 0.38 opacity is near-invisible and fails color-vision deficiency). Add non-color redundancy (e.g. 0-3 filled ring segments) while keeping current colors.

7. COPY (MoreTab.swift + DisclaimerCopy.swift + web): MoreTab.swift:48 hardcodes 'v0.2.0' — read CFBundleShortVersionString from Bundle instead. MoreTab.swift:90 'THE FOUR-DOMAIN FRAMEWORK' sits above five facets — change to 'THE FIVE FAS FACETS'. DisclaimerCopy.aboutNikolis (DisclaimerCopy.swift:36-46) describes a different four-domain taxonomy (Mechanical/Optical/Symmetry/Structural) — rewrite to describe the actual five-facet FAS and add a '// PLACEHOLDER — pending Dr Nikolis review' comment. DisclaimerCopy.swift:15-17 instructs calibrating via 'More → Calibrate landmarks' but that screen is a read-only explainer; correct the copy to describe the real path (the scope icon in a captured case's Analysis toolbar) without weakening the warning. Then sync web/content/disclaimer.ts verbatim with the final Swift copy (it is missing the calibration paragraph and its header comment cites a stale path — the source is now FaceMap/Resources/DisclaimerCopy.swift). Finally, web/content/metrics.ts documents only the 5 v0.1 metrics — add entries for surface displacement (Z-deficit, mm, facialShape facet), expression asymmetry (unitless ratio, expression facet), and photo-based skin quality (texture index, skinQuality facet), matching the Swift metric ids/names/targets in FaceMap/Analysis/Metrics/*.swift (read-only reference). Update its header comment.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme.swift facet hues exactly match web/lib/tokens.ts facetHues (lavender=skinQuality, slate=proportions, peach=expression) and both files carry mirror-sync comments
AestheticWheel renders five distinct 72° sectors; tapping each sector resolves to its own FaceDomain (Expression reachable); no overlapping labels at any rendered size including the PDF's 200pt frame

workOrders.title: Asset catalog, app icon (light/dark/tinted), and accent color
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap has NO asset catalog: no .xcassets exists anywhere in the repo, yet both build configurations set ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME = AppIcon (FaceMap.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:729 and :746), so the app ships with a blank icon and App Store submission would fail. You are the ONLY work order permitted to edit project.pbxproj (the project uses explicit PBXFileReference entries, objectVersion 77, no filesystem-synchronized groups — you must add the asset catalog reference and build-phase membership by hand or convert the relevant group carefully).

DELIVERABLES:
1. Create /Users/fuannegao/Documents/PERSONAL_WEBSITE/FACEMAP/FaceMap/Assets.xcassets with Contents.json ({"info":{"author":"xcode","version":1}}).
2. AppIcon.appiconset using the modern single-size form: a 1024x1024 universal iOS entry plus dark-appearance and tinted-appearance entries (appearances: [{appearance: luminosity, value: dark}] and [{appearance: luminosity, value: tinted}]).
3. ICON DESIGN (fully specified — generate programmatically, no human asset needed): the icon is the FAS wheel brand mark, no wordmark, no face imagery. Geometry mirrors web/components/brand-mark.tsx (read-only reference): full-bleed circle of radius 15/16 of half-width centered on canvas; central hub of radius 4/15 of the outer radius; FIVE 72° sectors starting at 12 o'clock, separated by gaps/slits of ~2.5% of canvas width in the background color; a thin ink ring (color 0x0A0A0F, ~3% width) around the outer circle; hub filled with the background color plus a hairline 0x0A0A0F ring. Sector colors in clockwise order from 12 o'clock (the unified FAS facet palette): 0xC9BBEE, 0xA6B4DD, 0x7A8094, 0xE9B5E0, 0xF2C9A1. Light variant: background 0xFAFAFA. Dark variant: same sectors on 0x0A0A0F background, hub 0x0A0A0F. Tinted variant: monochrome — sectors as white at opacity steps 0.38 / 0.52 / 0.64 / 0.82 / 1.0 (clockwise) on transparent/black per Apple tinted-icon guidance.
4. GENERATION: write a small generator script (e.g. scripts/generate_app_icon.swift using CoreGraphics, runnable via `swift scripts/generate_app_icon.swift`) that emits the three 1024x1024 PNGs into the appiconset; commit both script and PNGs. Verify the PNGs are non-empty and 1024x1024 (e.g. via sips).
5. AccentColor.colorset with universal color 0x0A0A0F, and set ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_GLOBAL_ACCENT_COLOR_NAME = AccentColor in both build configurations.
6. Wire Assets.xcassets into the FaceMap app target: PBXFileReference, group membership, and Resources build phase in project.pbxproj.

CONSTRAINTS: touch ONLY project.pbxproj, the new Assets.xcassets tree, and the new scripts/ file. Do not modify any Swift source. Do not change any other build setting. Verify with `xcodebuild -scheme FaceMap -destination 'generic/platform=iOS' build` (or simulator destination) that the catalog compiles and the build succeeds.
acceptanceCriteria: FaceMap/Assets.xcassets exists with valid Contents.json files (actool-parseable) and is a member of the app target's Resources build phase
AppIcon.appiconset contains light, dark, and tinted 1024x1024 PNGs matching the specified 5-sector wheel geometry and palette, generated by the committed script
AccentColor.colorset (0x0A0A0F) exists and ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_GLOBAL_ACCENT_COLOR_NAME = AccentColor is set in both configurations
xcodebuild build succeeds with no asset-catalog warnings about missing AppIcon
No Swift source files or unrelated build settings were modified

workOrders.title: First-run onboarding cards and versioned disclaimer acceptance
workOrders.mission:
FaceMap (SwiftUI iOS app for aesthetic practitioners) has no first-run experience: after the legal disclaimer gate, a new user lands on an empty patient list, then a camera with yaw-degree prompts, with nothing explaining the five-facet Facial Assessment Scale (FAS) that organizes the product — 'FAS' never appears in any UI string today. Separately, disclaimer acceptance is an unversioned boolean: DisclaimerGate.swift:4 stores @AppStorage("hasAcceptedDisclaimerV1") = true with no timestamp and no copy-version record, so when disclaimer text materially changes (it already did once), existing users never see the new terms — a weak audit trail for the app's main regulatory shield.

You may edit ONLY /Users/fuannegao/Documents/PERSONAL_WEBSITE/FACEMAP/FaceMap/UI/DisclaimerGate.swift. CONSTRAINT: do NOT create new .swift files (the Xcode project uses explicit file references and project.pbxproj is owned by another work order) — implement the onboarding views as additional types inside DisclaimerGate.swift. Do NOT edit DisclaimerCopy.swift (owned by another agent — reference its properties as they exist). The existing disclaimer text and the 'I am a licensed practitioner — continue' attestation flow must remain intact and blocking; the not-a-medical-device framing is non-negotiable.

1. VERSIONED ACCEPTANCE: introduce a `static let disclaimerVersion = 2` constant (with a comment: bump whenever DisclaimerCopy.firstLaunchBody materially changes) and persist {acceptedVersion: Int, acceptedAt: Date} (e.g. two @AppStorage keys 'disclaimerAcceptedVersion' and 'disclaimerAcceptedAt'). Migrate: if the legacy 'hasAcceptedDisclaimerV1' bool is true and no version is recorded, treat as version 1. Re-present the full disclaimer gate whenever storedVersion < disclaimerVersion. Keep the legacy key written for backward compatibility.

2. ONBOARDING CARDS: after first-time acceptance only (not on re-acceptance after a version bump, and skippable at any point), present a 3-card intro using existing design tokens (Theme/Type from FaceMap/UI/DesignSystem) and existing components where convenient (AestheticWheel, LogoMark are available): Card 1 — 'The Facial Assessment Scale': FaceMap organizes findings into five FAS facets (Skin Quality, Facial Shape, Proportions, Symmetry, Expression), each shown as a sector of the aesthetic wheel; severity is shown by ring depth, not a score. Card 2 — 'Three-pose capture': each visit captures a frontal and two oblique (±30°) TrueDepth scans plus clinical reference photos; the app coaches head position automatically. Card 3 — 'Calibrate once for meaningful values': metric values are not anatomically meaningful until landmark indices are calibrated on a captured case (Analysis screen → scope icon in the toolbar); until then a warning banner appears on every analysis. Wording must stay descriptive and instrument-like — no diagnostic or treatment-recommendation claims. Persist a 'hasSeenOnboarding' flag; add a way to replay later only if trivially possible from within DisclaimerGate (otherwise skip replay — More-tab entry is owned by another agent and is backlogged).
acceptanceCriteria: Fresh install: disclaimer gate → acceptance records version 2 + timestamp → 3 onboarding cards (skippable) → app; second launch shows neither

A device with only the legacy hasAcceptedDisclaimerV1 == true does NOT re-see the gate until disclaimerVersion is bumped above 1... (legacy bool maps to version 1, current version 2 triggers one re-presentation — verify this is the intended behavior and document the chosen mapping in a comment)
Bumping disclaimerVersion re-presents the full gate to previously accepted users without re-showing onboarding cards
Onboarding copy names the five FAS facets, the 3-pose capture, and the real calibration path (Analysis toolbar scope icon), with zero diagnostic/medical-claim language
All new UI uses Theme/Type tokens; only DisclaimerGate.swift was modified; project builds and existing tests pass

deviceTestScript:
# FaceMap On-Device Test Script (PM journey verification)

Device: iPhone with TrueDepth (X or newer), app freshly installed.

## 1. First run
1. Launch FaceMap. Confirm the disclaimer gate appears; tap "I am a licensed practitioner — continue".
2. Confirm you land on the Patients tab with the empty state. Note the copy promising an "Unassigned bucket you can re-bind later".

## 2. Patient-first journey (the blocker)
3. Tap "Add patient", create code `P-001`.
4. Open P-001 → tap "Add visit" → complete the 3-pose capture (frontal, oblique L, oblique R).
5. On the Analysis screen tap Save, enter label `Visit 1`, save.
6. EXPECTED BUG: go back to P-001's detail screen — it still says "No visits yet". Return to the Patients list: a patient named "Unassigned" now exists and contains Visit 1.
7. Try to find any way to move Visit 1 from Unassigned to P-001 (long-press the visit row, check Analysis toolbar, check menus). EXPECTED: none exists.

## 3. Stale capture state (cross-visit mixing)
8. Go to the Capture tab. Complete a full 3-pose session and reach Analysis WITHOUT saving; go back.
9. Re-enter the Capture flow and let it auto-capture once. EXPECTED BUG: it captures only Oblique R and immediately jumps to Analysis using the frontal mesh from the PREVIOUS session.

## 4. Comparison journey
10. Under the Unassigned patient (or P-001 if you worked around step 6), save a second visit.
11. In patient detail: tap ellipsis → "Compare visits" → select both visits → "Compare 2/2". Note how many taps this took.
12. In the CHANGES table, find "Surface displacement". EXPECTED BUG: value shown as a tiny percentage (e.g. "0.2%") instead of mm.
13. Look for any share/export button on the Compare screen. EXPECTED: none.

## 5. Settings traps
14. More → Settings → enable "Lock with Face ID" (authenticate). Force-quit and relaunch the app. EXPECTED BUG: app opens straight to patient data with no Face ID prompt.
15. Patients list: swipe-archive a patient. Try to find/restore it anywhere. EXPECTED: impossible.

## 6. Copy check
16. More tab footer should read v0.2.0 (stale); About screen says "FOUR-DOMAIN" above five listed domains; Settings sync says "Phase 2".
